heaviest_rainfall.status: 47.89mm of rain was recorded as having fallen on 09 November 2025, marking an exceptional 144.8 hour precipitation event.  At times the rain was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning activity recorded in a single day is 225, which occurred on 28 May 2026 (since our records began on 11 April 2025).

storm.status: No stormy weather is anticipated.  The last big storm was recorded on 18 July 2026, lasting 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The ongoing dry stretch, 18 days and counting started on 03 July 2026 and ranks 2nd in length since records started in 23 September 2024.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is no cold snap at the moment.  This year has experienced 3 cold snaps, with an average of 3.7 per year.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 27.7°C from 24 April 2026 (when the temperature reached 23.2°C but fell to -4.5°C).

heatwaves.status: There is currently a heatwave underway.  It started on 2026-07-01 and has lasted 20 days.  So far this year, there has been 3 heatwaves.  Typically, the yearly average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The previous tropical night was on 28 June 2026, with only 68 in total since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (209°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: To date, the maximum wind gust recorded is 128.2 mph on 27 January 2026.

run.status: The highest wind run ever seen is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: To date, the maximum wind speed recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 3.0°C) and outside temperature (down 2.6°C).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (up 8.7°C), feels like temperature (up 8.1°C), outside temperature (up 5.2°C), and outside humidity (up 13%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 633.4m higher than yesterday, 974.7m higher than the past week, 1541.9m higher than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 2200m to a maximum of 6530m, averaging 5260m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 4280m and 3720m respectively.
cloud levels.summary: The clouds will be high, plenty of sunshine is expected.

summary.summary: Inside; comfortable and pleasant temperatures. Outside; heat will be very noticeable, though not extreme. Also, air will feel comfortable and balanced and near calm conditions. A few light raindrops may fall, the clouds will be high, plenty of sunshine is expected, and very bright daylight. The weather will likely remain the same.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
